title: Chabertia cladophora
taxonomy: Chabertia cladophora belongs to the family Trichuridae, which is a group of nematode worms. This classification places it within the phylum Nematoda, a large and diverse group of roundworms. Chabertia cladophora is specifically a type of whipworm that infects animals, particularly ruminants and pigs. The species is further divided into various subspecies based on its geographical distribution and host specificity. This classification is essential for understanding the parasite's life cycle, behavior, and impact on its hosts.
